title: pinus contorta subsp. bolanderi
intro: Pinus contorta subsp. bolanderi, also known as Bolander's lodgepole pine, is a subspecies of evergreen tree native to the western United States and Canada. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial compounds. The health benefits of Bolander's lodgepole pine include reducing inflammation, fighting off infections, and providing antioxidant properties that can help protect against cell damage. The herbal preparations of this plant can be used to make teas, tinctures, and salves, and it is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
